title: Zammad Ticket Triage
summary: Student validates the basic Zammad ticket triage workflow, documentation quality, and instructor-review readiness.
student_goal: Review a help desk ticket, identify whether required documentation is present, and produce a clear resolution summary that another technician could understand.

required_evidence.label: Ticket review completed
required_evidence.description: Student must identify the ticket number, issue summary, requester/customer, and current ticket state.

required_evidence.label: Resolution summary
required_evidence.description: Student must include a clear resolution or status summary in help desk language.

required_evidence.label: Training-ticket workflow confirmed
required_evidence.description: Student must confirm that the ticket was reviewed in Zammad, evidence was checked in ARIA, and the final note is ready for instructor review.

student_workflow: Open ARIA AI Mentor and load the ticket workflow.
Review the ticket number, requester/customer, issue summary, and current state.
Write a clear help desk summary with evidence and next step.
Do not close the ticket unless the instructor specifically tells you to close it.
opening_questions: What is the issue summary and current ticket state?
What evidence or note did you add to the ticket workflow?
Is the final note clear enough for another technician to understand?
completion_signals: final note ready for instructor review
mentor_guidance_rules: Do not mark completion unless required evidence is present.
Do not treat Zammad ticket state as the same as ARIA training completion.
If evidence is missing, ask for the missing documentation rather than giving a final pass.
Require clear help desk language, not vague completion statements.
